claim: Gradio 5 enables streaming chat interfaces with LLMs using generator functions that yield chunks to UI in real-time
actionable: Use Gradio generators to build responsive chat UIs that show token-by-token output as it streams

claim: Chat interfaces built with Gradio automatically maintain conversation history without manual state management
actionable: Pass message history to Gradio chat interface; Gradio handles persistence automatically

claim: System message handling differs between OpenAI (ML chat format) and Anthropic (inserted in call); abstraction via Gradio handles both
actionable: Use Gradio abstraction when switching between LLM providers to avoid rewriting system message logic

claim: Gradio 5 supports arbitrary provider APIs via OpenAI format compatibility; useful for Together AI, Grok, etc.
actionable: For multi-provider support, use OpenAI format with custom base URLs to test different LLM backends via single Gradio interface

claim: Gradio UI can be shared publicly via share=True parameter without requiring separate hosting
actionable: Use Gradio share feature for rapid prototyping and gathering feedback without deploying to servers
